name: Customer signs the contract on a phone
description: After accepting a quote, customer receives a contract email. They open it, scroll the terms, draw a signature, and submit. Contractor sees the signed contract and can move to invoice.

persona.name: Asha Patel
persona.context: Accepted Diego's quote yesterday. Today gets a contract to sign.

preconditions: Diego's account triggered contract send for the won quote
Contract has terms: scope, deposit %, schedule, exclusions

steps.expect: Subject mentions contract; CTA 'Review and sign'

steps.action: Taps the CTA on iPhone Safari
steps.expect: Public contract page renders: full terms readable; signature area at the bottom with a 'Sign' button
Sticky 'Sign' affordance once user has reached the bottom; no scroll hijack

steps.action: Taps Sign and draws signature with finger
steps.expect: Signature canvas accepts touch input; Submit becomes enabled only after a signature is drawn
Confirmation 'Signed — Diego will send the deposit invoice next.' Customer can download a PDF copy
Contract status flips to signed; activity feed entry; bell notification

emails.subject: Your contract from Diego — fence repair
emails.expect: Email body summarizes scope and deposit so customer knows what they're signing before opening

evaluationCriteria.check: Contract page reads like a clean document, not a CRM screen
Signing is one continuous action — not a multi-step form
Submitted signature is stored as both image and structured event with timestamp + IP for audit
Signature canvas has a text alternative (typed full name) for users who can't draw
